feed.title: Euronews
feed.subtitle: Combined Euronews feed: News, My Europe, No Comment, Culture, Sport, Travel, and Next.

entry.title: Peppa Pig backlash as US company Hasbro requires child actors to sign voices over to AI
entry.content: US entertainment giant Hasbro is reportedly asking child actors on the popular series Peppa Pig to sign over their voices to artificial intelligence under new contract terms. An open letter signed by 1,000 industry professionals condemns the AI contract clause and warns of matters of consent.

entry.title: Amid shifts in energy supply, which EU countries consume and produce the most gas?
entry.content: Natural gas demand is climbing across the EU, even as domestic production struggles to keep pace. How do member states compare?

entry.title: Tehran will allow nuclear inspections during peace talks, former Iranian diplomat says
entry.content: Seyed Hossein Mousavian, former spokesman for Iran's nuclear negotiation team, tells Euronews he believes Tehran will allow the IAEA access to its nuclear sites as part of negotiations with the US, despite some Iranian officials saying access could only be granted after a final deal has been struck.

entry.title: Turkey produces last minute winner to beat the US as Germany end win streak with loss to Ecuador
entry.content: The final matchday saw teams take on both more cautious and reckless approaches as they looked to secure qualification into the knockout stages.

entry.title: UK foreign policy to remain unchanged despite government transition, deputy PM Lammy says
entry.content: The UK's deputy Prime Minister David Lammy has told Europe Today that his country's foreign policy — including its support for Ukraine and efforts to restore EU ties — will remain unchanged when a new Prime Minister takes the reins after Keir Starmer's exit.

entry.title: Historic joy in Abidjan as Ivory Coast reach World Cup knockouts for first time
entry.content: Ivory Coast reaches World Cup knockout stage for the first time after Nicolas Pépé’s brace beats Curaçao 2-0, sparking huge celebrations in Abidjan.

entry.title: Newsletter: Temperatures rise over sanctions against Russia
entry.content: In this newsletter: Bulgaria attempts to bulldoze part of the EU's 21st sanctions package against Russia, criticism mounts over the EU's hyped rule of law reports, and a baby makes an appearance at a council meeting in Luxembourg.

entry.title: Japan fans flood Shibuya as World Cup draw with Sweden sets up Brazil clash
entry.content: Japan reach World Cup last 32 after 1-1 draw with Sweden on 26 June, prompting celebrations across Tokyo. , sparking huge celebrations in Tokyo’s Shibuya. They now face Brazil in the knockout stage.

entry.title: Harvey Weinstein's New York rape charge dropped - accuser says she can't endure a fourth trial
entry.content: Weinstein still stands convicted of another sexual felony in New York and others in California. He remains behind bars at New York's maximum-security prison, Rikers Island.

entry.title: EU releases €3 billion loan for Ukraine as US-Iran talks remain fragile
entry.content: Ukraine has received 3.2 billion euros as the first disbursement under the European Union's 90 billion euro support loan. Meanwhile, Iranians are rejecting claims that the UN would visit nuclear enrichment sites in the country.

entry.title: How Portugal's Alentejo region has become a hotspot for luxury hotel openings
entry.content: New openings, villa relaunches and stylish boutique stays are drawing fresh attention to Portugal’s Alentejo region, from Comporta and Melides to the historic streets of Évora.

entry.title: Euronews Culture's Film of the Week: 'Supergirl' - Save the puppy, save the world
entry.content: After a strong start to the rebooted DC Universe last year with 'Superman' comes a new space adventure which follows the Man of Steel's chaotic cousin... And her super-powered pup.

entry.title: Gulf states weigh living side by side with Iran as Tehran weaponises Hormuz
entry.content: As the frontline countries paying the largest price for the Iran war, pressed by severe economic urgencies, the Gulf states are beginning their own firm process towards regional stability with rival Iran, while Tehran continues weaponising the Strait of Hormuz to sow division with its neighbours.

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | June 26th, 2026 – Morning
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 26th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: Côte d’Ivoire: Discover West Africa’s beach and culture hotspot
entry.content: Regarded as West Africa’s answer to the Caribbean, Côte d’Ivoire has an extraordinary coast, UNESCO-listed culture and direct flights from Europe.

entry.title: Europe’s Taliban Dilemma: the struggle to return Afghan migrants
entry.content: EU countries are engaging with Afghanistan’s Taliban-led regime to return irregular migrants deemed security threats or convicted of serious offences. Yet any such arrangement could carry political and diplomatic costs, requiring concessions and risking public backlash.

entry.title: Death toll from Venezuela earthquakes rises to 235, search and rescue efforts continue
entry.content: The two quakes struck in quick succession, just minutes apart, according to the US Geological Survey, marking it one of the South American nation’s worst disasters in over a century.

entry.title: US Supreme Court clears way for mass deportations of Haitians and Syrians
entry.content: The 6-3 ruling hands Donald Trump a sweeping victory on immigration, stripping legal protections from hundreds of thousands of people the State Department warns would be returning to dangerous countries.

entry.title: EU's door 'is open to UK', fomer Brexit negotiator Barnier tells Euronews
entry.content: Ten years following the landmark vote, the EU's former Brexit negotiator said the bloc's door is open to the UK, but that London "cannot cherry-pick" EU policies.

entry.title: Football for friends: Winning ways World Cup supporters have gone viral – for the right reasons
entry.content: Food discoveries, tidy stadiums and bagpipes on the streets of Boston are only the beginning as football fans across the United States, Canada and Mexico have been leaving very distinctive signs of their presence...

entry.title: Cargo ship hit by projectile in Hormuz as Iran issues renewed passage warning
entry.content: The British military said a cargo ship on the new Omani UN-backed route sustained bridge damage from a projectile, hours after the IRGC warned that vessels without Iranian permission were transiting "illegally".

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | June 25th, 2026 – Evening
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 25th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: Heat stress threatens 130 million European workers, trade union body warns
entry.content: The European Trade Union Institute (ETUI) has presented a proposal for European legislation to protect workers' health during heatwaves.

entry.title: NATO chief Rutte defends allies as Trump says he was 'let down' on Iran
entry.content: Rutte pushed back gently against Trump's latest NATO complaints, saying, “I know there have been isolated cases about which you are really disappointed, but generally speaking, your European allies have been there with you."

entry.title: 'I'm in great shape,' boxing legend Floyd Mayweather tells Euronews ahead of Athens fight
entry.content: Floyd "Money" Mayweather is set to face Greek fighter Mike Zambidis in Athens on Saturday 27 June.

entry.title: La Guaira, Venezuela’s port city, shows extensive earthquake damage after the quake
entry.content: New images from La Guaira on 25 June revealed widespread destruction after twin earthquakes struck Venezuela's Caribbean coast, damaging buildings, roads and key public infrastructure.

entry.title: Brussels tightens rules on US cloud providers as it pushes for tech sovereignty
entry.content: The European Commission has decided that Microsoft Azure and Amazon Web Services should face strict rules to improve cloud competition. The move risks angering Washington as Brussels pushes the European tech sovereignty agenda.

entry.title: James Bond search enters final stage with planned August screen tests
entry.content: Production on Bond 26 is set to begin next year. The expectation is that the official announcement for the next 007 actor will happen by the end of 2026.

entry.title: Laissons Parler Les Gens: Podcast addresses issues facing young Africans online
entry.content: Youssoupha acknowledged cyberbullying and harassment were part of the challenges facing young Africans today, but he urged the youth to focus on the positives.

entry.title: Moment French navy boards suspected Russian 'ghost fleet' tanker
entry.content: The French navy boarded a tanker suspected of belonging to Russia's so-called "ghost fleet" in the Mediterranean, the fifth such operation since September 2025, as Paris steps up efforts to enforce sanctions linked to the war in Ukraine.

entry.title: Leonard Cohen estate slams use of 'Hallelujah' at Trump's Freedom 250 rally
entry.content: Donald Trump had announced earlier in June that 'Hallelujah' would be performed at the opening of the Great American State Fair, fuelling the long-term feud between the US president and the estate of Leonard Cohen.

entry.title: Central Asia’s first Paragliding World Cup is taking place in Kazakhstan
entry.content: Kazakhstan’s Zhetysu region is hosting the first Paragliding World Cup in Central Asia, bringing together around 150 pilots from 33 countries. The main discipline of the World Cup is cross-country flying.

entry.title: IRGC warns ships off Oman's corridor in Hormuz as Rubio draws line on tolls
entry.content: The IRGC warned that any vessel navigating outside Tehran's designated routes was acting illegally, directly rejecting Oman's newly announced temporary corridor, as Rubio told a GCC meeting the US would not accept fees regardless of how they are framed.

entry.title: How much do we really know about microplastics? | Euronews Tech Talks
entry.content: Microplastics pollute our planet, raising questions about their impact on human health and the environment. This Q&A episode of Euronews Tech Talks sums up what science knows, and does not know, about them.

entry.title: International community pledges aid to Venezuela after devastating earthquakes
entry.content: The United States Geological Survey has estimated that the final death toll could be between 10,000 and 100,000 people.

entry.title: Bigger than Jupiter, lighter than cotton candy: Astronomers discover two ‘super-puff’ planets
entry.content: The two planets orbiting a star 1,110 light-years away are the biggest exoplanets ever found with a density lower than cotton candy, and may be mostly made of hydrogen and helium.

entry.title: Europe summer travel trends: Short breaks, family holidays and coolcations drive demand
entry.content: From quick getaways to heatwave-avoiding escapes, new data from Trip.com reveals the trends that are shaping summer travel across Europe in 2026.

entry.title: Mediterranean travel trends: How ‘blue mind experiences’ and local culture keep people coming back
entry.content: As fly-and-flop holidays fall out of fashion, travellers are looking for more meaningful travel experiences even while prioritising sun and sea.

entry.title: SpaceX has quietly launched a spacecraft almost no one knew existed
entry.content: Rumours have circulated that it could be used to transport military hardware.

entry.title: Phoebe Bridgers announces new album after solo hiatus
entry.content: The American singer is coming back with her third solo record 'Lost Weekend' ahead of a US and European tour starting in September.

entry.title: US-Iran talks could derail fragile peace process, says ex-Israeli spokesperson
entry.content: Former Israeli government spokesperson Eylon Levy believes Israel's campaign is "creating the urgency and opportunity" for Lebanon to confront Hezbollah. Levy said that if Lebanon is "capable of dismantling Hezbollah", it could mark the beginning of "a beautiful peace between Israel and Lebanon.

entry.title: Macron says France intercepted a Russian shadow fleet oil tanker off coast of Sicily
entry.content: It comes after the UK announced earlier this month that it had intercepted and boarded the Russian shadow fleet tanker "Smyrtos" in the English Channel.

entry.title: Qatar fans hopeful about the future despite World Cup exit after Bosnia defeat
entry.content: Qatar's World Cup dream came to an end after three group matches, with fans in Doha reflecting on the team's strong performance while remaining optimistic about the future, as Qatar emerges from the war with hope.

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | June 25th, 2026 – Midday
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 25th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: Two years after Gen Z uprising, Kenyan protesters return to Nairobi streets
entry.content: Two years after Kenya's 2024 Gen Z tax revolt shook the nation, protesters gathered in Nairobi on 25 June to seek justice for victims and press for greater government accountability.

entry.title: Eleven EU countries call for methane rules pause amid energy security fears
entry.content: The eleven EU countries say that the methane rules could jeopardise natural gas supply, especially from foreign suppliers who would face extra costs. Environmentalists warn the EU not to cave in to pressure while the United Nations urged world nations to do more to mitigate methane emissions.

entry.title: Ukraine gets €3.2 billion from EU support loan, von der Leyen says
entry.content: The announcement marks the first disbursement under the €90 billion loan. A second payment worth about €6 billion and dedicated to drone production is expected to be unveiled in the coming days.

entry.title: Pro-Israel Spanish journalist Pilar Rahola accused of complicity in genocide
entry.content: Barcelona’s public prosecutor has opened proceedings against Rahola over allegations of incitement to hatred and complicity in genocide.

entry.title: Fact check: Viral drone interception video shows Russia's Yolka system
entry.content: A video widely shared on social media appears to show a handheld device intercepting a drone. While some users have hailed the system as a Ukrainian military innovation, others have claimed it is Russian technology. The Cube traced the footage to determine the origin and purpose of the device.

entry.title: Pay transparency isn't only about women. It exposes inequalities affecting millions of workers
entry.content: The EU's pay transparency rules can help close the gender pay gap and expose how Europe systematically underpays young, migrant, disabled and racialised workers.

entry.title: North Korean soldier suspected of defecting crosses demilitarised zone into South Korea
entry.content: According to data from South Korea's Ministry of Unification, more than 34,000 North Koreans have defected to the South since its records began in 1998.

entry.title: AmaRudi: Europe's largest river cruise ship to launch on the Danube next spring
entry.content: The AmaRudi, AmaWaterways’ second double-width vessel, will accommodate 196 guests across 98 staterooms and suites when it launches in April 2027.

entry.title: Higher penalties, more time to prosecute: Child sexual abuse victims welcome new EU directive
entry.content: New rules greenlighted this week will cover the use of AI in child abuse cases and considerably extend the limitation period to a minimum of 32 years.

entry.title: Heatwave scorches Europe from London to Madrid as temperatures near 39C
entry.content: A powerful heatwave swept across Europe, stretching from France to Spain and Belgium and pushing temperatures towards 39C, raising concerns about public safety and pressure on infrastructure.

entry.title: Moment earthquake hits Venezuela, sending people fleeing in panic
entry.content: Images showing cracked walls, collapsed ceiling panels and debris strewn across parts of Simón Bolívar International Airport highlighted the scale of damage after two powerful earthquakes struck Venezuela on 24 June 2026.

entry.title: Trump says 'courageous' Zelenskyy is 'doing pretty well' in war with Russia
entry.content: "You have to say he's courageous, he's got great equipment, he's got great men, he's got fighters," Trump said of Zelenskyy.

entry.title: Newsletter: Can the Recovery Conference mend a fraying partnership?
entry.content: In today's newsletter: A high-level conference in Gdańsk has been plagued by no-shows from the Ukrainian government, NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte unveils "Trump Trillion" charts while at the Oval Office, and a Commissioner heads to Malta to address corruption.

entry.title: Popular Ibiza DJ named as alleged 'mastermind' behind international drug trafficking operation
entry.content: Popular Ibiza DJ Hugo Bianco has been named in a major Spain-US drug trafficking case. He is the alleged “mastermind” of an international drug trafficking network, and faces a nine-and-a-half prison sentence.

entry.title: Mexico, Switzerland and Brazil top their groups after final matchday of World Cup group stages
entry.content: Brazilian superstar Neymar Jr made an emotional World Cup debut after earlier doubts saw him on the cusp of missing out due to a recent string of injuries and unconvincing performances.

entry.title: Europe Today: Ukraine seeks defence, energy deals in Poland as Macron meets Meloni in France
entry.content: The Ukraine Recovery Conference 2026 kicks off today in Gdańsk, with Kyiv aiming to secure deals on defence capabilities, energy and economic cooperation. Meanwhile, France’s President Emmanuel Macron welcomes Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni to the French Riviera today for a major summit.

entry.title: Which EU countries fight pay gaps? New transparency rules split the bloc
entry.content: Only four EU countries have implemented the EU’s Pay Transparency Directive, missing the June deadline meant to boost enforcement of equal pay rules across the bloc. Watch the video.

entry.title: Mumbai monsoon floods streets and disrupts travel
entry.content: Heavy monsoon rains flood Mumbai, submerging roads, disrupting traffic and forcing residents to wade through waterlogged streets in India’s financial hub.

entry.title: Global Beatles Day: Who was the walrus?
entry.content: On the inaugural Global Beatles Day, we unpack one of the most enduring mysteries surrounding the legendary band...

entry.title: Washing underwear in kettles and raiding the breakfast buffet: Strange hotel guest habits revealed
entry.content: Using kettles to wash underwear, smuggling food from the breakfast buffet and squeezing extra guests into rooms are among the hotel habits Britons have admitted to in a new study, which comes alongside a new etiquette guide from Hotels.com and manners expert William Hanson.

entry.title: France's largest startup campus Station F turns nine. Here's why it's nervous about the election
entry.content: The biggest threat to France's startup scene isn't the market or AI. It's the ballot box, according to data from startup incubator Station F.

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | June 25th, 2026 – Morning
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 25th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: No Zelenskyy in Gdansk: can Ukraine’s recovery forum ease Warsaw–Kyiv tensions?
entry.content: The Ukraine Recovery Conference opens in Gdansk on Thursday amid an unprecedented spat between Kyiv and Warsaw. With both presidents absent, attention will focus not only on reconstruction initiatives but also on efforts to ease increasingly strained bilateral relations.

entry.title: At least 32 killed and 700 injured in powerful Venezuela earthquakes
entry.content: Residents of the capital Caracas were forced to evacuate their residences and seek shelter outdoors as the quakes, which struck less than 200 km from the capital, caused widespread damage.

entry.title: Hormuz confusion deepens as Iran and US clash over who controls it
entry.content: Trump said Tehran had promised no tolls or charges in the strait, hours after Iran and Oman announced a joint permit system asserting their authority over the waterway. Rubio flew to Abu Dhabi to reassure Gulf allies, as analysts warned control over Hormuz would be a major strategic victory for Iran

entry.title: Catholics walk barefoot in mud ritual
entry.content: Devotees in rice paddies cover themselves in mud and banana leaves for the Taong Putik festival, honouring St. John the Baptist with barefoot processions.

entry.title: Scientists find human DNA over 2,000 years old in caves in Spain and Portugal
entry.content: A research team based in Cáceres has found human DNA more than 2,000 years old on cave walls in Spain and Portugal, a discovery that could transform the study of prehistory.

entry.title: Has Brexit made Europe stronger or weaker? MEPs go face-to-face on The Ring
entry.content: What has Brexit taught Europe about sovereignty, migration, economic competitiveness, democratic accountability, and disinformation? Has it strengthened the case for deeper European integration? MEPs debate the future of EU-UK relations on this edition of The Ring.

entry.title: UK records hottest June day ever as Europe swelters in heatwave
entry.content: The Met Office has warned that "exceptionally warm" temperatures are expected to continue through the middle of the week.

entry.title: Europe's five largest military powers meet ahead of key NATO summit with Ukraine in mind
entry.content: The leaders of Europe's largest military powers met in Berlin on Wednesday in preparation for a pivotal NATO summit next month, to discuss support and security guarantees for Ukraine and how to strengthen the Atlantic Alliance's European Pillar.

entry.title: German authorities carry out raids over alleged Russian gas sabotage
entry.content: An investigation is looking into the sale of Gazprom's Berlin-based subsidiary Gazprom Germania, which took place around a month after Russia launched its full-scale invasion of Ukraine in February 2022.

entry.title: Special Report: Brexit, ten years on: could the UK ever rejoin the EU?
entry.content: Today's Special Report is anchored by Mared Gwyn and features correspondents Maria Tadeo and Shona Murray, alongside top interview guests, for a deep dive into one of the most consequential political decisions in modern European history: Brexit and the United Kingdom's departure from the EU.

entry.title: IMF to closely monitor China’s economic growth amid trade tensions
entry.content: An accurate measurement of China's digital economy is a priority for other powers competing with Beijing in fast-developing technologies.

entry.title: London's Met police to scale up use of drones and live facial recognition
entry.content: UK civil liberties campaign group Big Brother Watch has criticised the use of technologies such as drones and facial recognition.

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | June 24th, 2026 – Evening
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 24th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: Portugal's World Cup victory sparks celebrations in Lisbon
entry.content: Thousands of fans in Lisbon celebrated Portugal's 5-0 World Cup victory over Uzbekistan, with Cristiano Ronaldo scoring twice and becoming the first player to score in six different World Cup tournaments.

entry.title: Paris's Cirque d'Hiver begins renovation to restore its original splendour
entry.content: Twenty ornamental frescoes from the Second Empire are to be restored over twelve to eighteen months. The seating, the polychrome ceiling canvas and the stained-glass windows will also be refurbished.

entry.title: First whales killed as Iceland restarts commercial hunt
entry.content: Iceland resumes commercial whaling after a two-year pause, with two fin whales processed at the Hvalur facility, reigniting controversy and protests.

entry.title: Two workers for a Norwegian demining charity killed in Russian strike on Ukraine
entry.content: The NPA has more than 450 staff based in Ukraine, where it has been carrying out mine clearance activities since 2023.

entry.title: Madrid, Woody Allen's new cinematic muse
entry.content: Woody Allen’s next film, set entirely in Madrid, will treat the city as a star. Filming begins in October, following in the footsteps of New York, Paris, Rome and Barcelona.

entry.title: European heatwave forces Eiffel Tower early closure and tourist attraction shutdowns
entry.content: From the Eiffel Tower and the Louvre to Buckingham Palace's Changing of the Guard, soaring temperatures are forcing attractions to close, shorten opening hours and cancel events as a heatwave grips Europe.

entry.title: EU diplomatic service announces new top posts with strong defence focus
entry.content: The EEAS has named Kajsa Ollongren as secretary general, with Matti Maasikas and David Cvach in deputy roles. The defence-focused appointments come as the bloc's foreign policy machinery faces increasing pressure.

entry.title: Tehran labels framework deal 'US defeat' as Trump threatens to halt talks
entry.content: Iranian key negotiator hailed the US–Iran framework deal as a “declaration of US defeat,” while Trump warned that talks would end immediately if reports that Tehran is seeking to impose tolls on ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz prove to be true.

entry.title: EU scales back Ukraine's accession timetable due to Hungarian resistance
entry.content: Faced with renewed resistance from Hungary, the EU has been forced to change plans and now aims to unblock two, rather than five, accession clusters with Ukraine and Moldova before the summer break.

entry.title: Senior UK minister rules out leadership challenge as Burnham edges closer to Downing Street
entry.content: Jones' decision removes a major obstacle standing between Burnham and the UK's top job and could mean the latter is in Downing Street as early as July.

entry.title: Evo Morales warns of civil war from Chapare stronghold: 'I will not surrender'
entry.content: Evo Morales speaks out from hiding in the Chapare jungle as Bolivia enters its seventh week of fuel, food and medicine shortages.

entry.title: Didier Deschamps to miss France's match against Norway following the death of his mother
entry.content: The France head coach has returned to France to attend his mother’s funeral. He is set to return to Boston on Saturday, the day after the French team's game against Norway.

entry.title: National Portrait Gallery removes artwork after row over Churchill's legacy
entry.content: The London-based museum has faced controversy over a video installation blaming Winston Churchill for a colonial-era famine in India.

entry.title: Zuckerberg wants Meta to launch its own prediction market, report says
entry.content: Prediction platforms Polymarket and Kalshi have made record profits this year, and now Mark Zuckerberg has reportedly directed tech giant Meta to build their own.

entry.title: The ‘Aperol Spritz index’: European cities to visit for an affordable aperitivo
entry.content: If you love the bright orange beverage but don’t love the price tag attached to it in many bars, here’s where you should be heading.

entry.title: Meloni-Trump spat: What do the polls say about Meloni's popularity?
entry.content: A dispute between Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni and US President Donald Trump has sparked debate about her standing with voters.

entry.title: Greek Supreme Court rejects Laura Kövesi appeal over EU prosecutors' terms
entry.content: Greece's top court ruled the EPPO lacked legal standing to challenge a domestic decision to limit prosecutors' terms to two years instead of five.

entry.title: Giesinger celebrates Munich beer culture, pushes for Oktoberfest place
entry.content: Munich Brewers’ Day 2026 brings processions, ceremony and beer celebrations to Marienplatz and Viktualienmarkt, honouring the city’s brewing guild tradition.

entry.title: Consenting adults: Cate Blanchett launches project to allow artists to choose AI identities
entry.content: Hollywood heavyweight Cate Blanchett is behind a new free online tool which allows artists to give or withhold permission for their image to be used by artificial intelligence.

entry.title: Stars including Cate Blanchett and Javier Bardem unite to protect their identities in AI era
entry.content: A new free tool allows artists to give or withhold consent for their image to be used by artificial intelligence.

entry.title: Hollywood stars oppose AI: how a new register protects artists' identities
entry.content: A new free tool allows artists to decide whether or not to give the green light for their image to be used by artificial intelligence.

entry.title: Ten years after Brexit, travel rules confusion remains widespread
entry.content: From passport validity rules and healthcare cover to new EU border checks and visa requirements, many British travellers are still unsure what Brexit means for their European holidays.

entry.title: Trump vs algae: Viral reactions as fencing goes up around Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool
entry.content: The Trump administration has been losing a war with algae in the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool. Critics think it’s the perfect metaphor for Trump’s tenure as president, and the latest move to cover up the embarrassment has sent the internet into meltdown.

entry.title: UAE trains connecting Abu Dhabi and Dubai set to launch this September
entry.content: Etihad Rail will start its route between the UAE capital and Fujairah in late June, with further stations opening in the coming months.

entry.title: Gulf banks want AI, but can they keep control of customer data?
entry.content: As banks in the Gulf explore new AI tools, the focus is increasingly shifting from what the technology can do to how it can be used responsibly.

entry.title: Heatwave and wildfire alerts, power cuts in Finistère: France swelters
entry.content: Temperatures of 40 to 45C are forecast in France on Wednesday 24 June, wildfire alerts are in force in the centre and 68,000 homes in Finistère are without power.

entry.title: World's fastest supercomputer is now from China, surpassing US and Germany
entry.content: One of the most interesting use cases for the supercomputer is that it is key to developing models used in artificial intelligence (AI).

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | June 24th, 2026 – Midday
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 24th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: Twelve EU countries seek green funds beyond 2030 to cope with energy transition
entry.content: The member states are historically more dependent on fossil fuels than other EU countries, and have benefited since 2021 from special funding to cope with the accelerating shift to renewable energy sources.

entry.title: Cate Blanchett launches free tool to help people protect identity from AI
entry.content: The acclaimed Australian actor presented her Human Consent Registry at the European Parliament. The tool allows people to protect their likeness from being used by AII.

entry.title: Kazakhstan signs €10bn in deals with Europe as Tokayev wraps Brussels visit
entry.content: During President Kassym-Jomart Tokayev's visit to Brussels, strategic documents were signed with the EU, and 30 commercial deals were concluded with EU companies, with the EU being the Central Asian country's largest trading and investment partner.

entry.title: Paris neighborhood fighting the heatwave with with 'Ciné-clim' initiative
entry.content: In order to combat the days’ highest temperature peaks, one cultural initiative in Paris has everyone asking: “Why isn’t this nation-wide?”

entry.title: Seal at Hungarian zoo brushes teeth
entry.content: A seal at Nyíregyháza Zoo in Hungary brushes its teeth in the Arctic Ice World exhibit, showcasing daily care and animal enrichment activities.

entry.title: Rutte meets Trump to defuse tensions ahead of major NATO summit
entry.content: The Secretary General's meeting with Trump comes ahead of a potentially bruising leaders' summit in July. Relations between the Trump administration and Europe remain fractious over the war in Iran, with the US cutting its European defence commitments drastically.

entry.title: Pedro Sánchez tells Congress: 'I never knew of, nor would I have tolerated, any of these practices'
entry.content: Spain's prime minister faced opposition calls to resign as he defended his government over a string of corruption cases involving his party, partner and brother.

entry.title: Anthropic's Mythos AI found flaws in classified US systems within hours, officials say
entry.content: Mere hours, not weeks — that is how long it took an Anthropic AI model to find vulnerabilities across classified US government systems.

entry.title: Péter Magyar quotes Donald Tusk: 'The V4 is back'
entry.content: At the Grassalkovich Palace in Gödöllő, the Hungarian prime minister opened the post‑Visegrád Four talks press conference with the Polish PM’s Brussels remark.

entry.title: Dead tree painted red to highlight climate change impact
entry.content: A painted red oak at Kew Gardens, killed in the 2022 heatwave, stands beside a resilient “Tree of Hope”, highlighting climate change impacts in the UK.

entry.title: Thailand’s ‘Michael Jackson’ jailed over fatal drunk-driving crash
entry.content: A criminal court in Bangkok has sentenced Manasawin Nantasen, known as Tik Shiro, to two years in prison for "drunk driving resulting in loss of life".

entry.title: Iran stronger after conflict, warns former Iran negotiator Wendy Sherman
entry.content: In an interview with Euronews’ flagship morning show Europe Today, former US diplomat Wendy Sherman welcomes the current talks aimed at ending the US-Iran conflict between but warns that Tehran appears to be in a stronger position.

entry.title: ‘Reality check’ for England: Is it time to unleash Saka and Rashford?
entry.content: Head coach Thomas Tuchel urges England fans to remain positive after the Three Lions were held to a frustrating goalless draw by Ghana in World Cup group L.

entry.title: IAEA chief says inspections of Iran's nuclear sites 'going to happen'
entry.content: IAEA's Grossi pushed back against Tehran's claim that no agreement has been reached to allow inspectors to inspect bombed enrichment sites, citing the signed US-Iran framework deal.

entry.title: IAEA chief says nuclear inspections of Iran's enrichment sites 'going to happen' despite Tehran push
entry.content: Rafael Mariano Grossi cited the signed US-Iran memorandum of understanding, pushing back against Tehran's claim that bombed enrichment sites are off-limits to inspectors.

entry.title: Europeans want less dependence on foreign technologies, study finds
entry.content: Europe relies heavily on foreign tech, but citizens increasingly want EU-controlled alternatives. How willing are Europeans to pay the price for digital sovereignty?

entry.title: Toronto streets fill with Panama and Croatia supporters
entry.content: Supporters of Panama and Croatia turned downtown Toronto into a sea of flags and chants ahead of their crucial World Cup Group L clash.

entry.title: 'The Blair Witch Project’ movie reboot will haunt cinemas in 2027
entry.content: The reimagining of the horror feature that became a worldwide sensation in 1999 is set to be released next year. Can it succeed where previous sequels failed?

entry.title: Ronaldo shines as Portugal deliver 5-0 thrashing of Uzbekistan and Engand suffer 0-0 draw to Ghana
entry.content: Ronaldo was quick to shutdown criticism over his goalless World Cup opener and overall usefulness to his national side as he grabbed a brace in Portugal’s 5-0 pounding of Uzbekistan.

entry.title: Newsletter: Ukraine tensions and the Taliban question
entry.content: In today's newsletter: All eyes are on Berlin to see if Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy and Polish Prime Minister Donald Tusk can resolve tensions between the capitals, and further questions loom regarding a meeting between the European Commission and the Taliban on Tuesday.

entry.title: Rock in Rio 2026: Live music still draws thousands
entry.content: The opening weekend of Rock in Rio drew thousands of visitors from 125 countries, at a time when music and live shows fit into a mobile phone.

entry.title: Europe Today: Sánchez under pressure as Europe backs Ukraine
entry.content: Europe's five leading military powers meet in Berlin to discuss support for Ukraine and tougher sanctions on Russia, while US-Iran negotiations continue in Switzerland. Meanwhile, Spain's Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez faces pressure over a widening corruption scandal.

entry.title: Romeo Beckham to make acting debut in queer tennis film ‘Forty Love’
entry.content: After pursuing careers in football, tennis and modelling, 23-year-old Romeo Beckham is making his acting debut in a new French film, set to be released later this year.

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | June 24th, 2026 – Morning
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 24th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: Europe's best hotel swimming pools: Take a dip and stay cool in the heatwave
entry.content: Europe boasts some of the world’s most stunning hotel swimming pools. From Italy to Iceland, we take a look at the perfect spots to keep cool during the summer months.

entry.title: From misting fans to wearable air conditioners: Here’s the tech to keep you cool this summer
entry.content: Euronews Next takes a look at some of the tech to keep you cool this summer.

entry.title: New giraffe born in Denmark zoo
entry.content: At Copenhagen Zoo, a giraffe calf is born as staff and visitors witness the moment. The mother immediately cares for her newborn.

entry.title: US eases restrictions on Iranian national football team allowing travel two days before next match
entry.content: The US Department of Homeland Security says it had always intended on easing restrictions on the Iranian team after ensuring that safety protocols were up to order.

entry.title: Technical malfunction with communications sytem temporarily halts entire German railway network
entry.content: Deutsche Bahn said its services were resuming “step by step” after an almost three hour outage which froze all of its operations nationwide.

entry.title: Airbus under fresh scrutiny as EASA orders A380 inspections over wing cracks
entry.content: The aviation giant is already battling supply chain problems and growing costs across its operations.

entry.title: Cristiano Ronaldo scores at his sixth World Cup
entry.content: Cristiano Ronaldo scored as Portugal beat Uzbekistan in their World Cup group match in Houston, becoming the first footballer to score at six World Cup tournaments.

entry.title: Giant Messi statue honors Argentina's World Cup hero
entry.content: A giant mural near Buenos Aires and a towering statue in Patagonia highlight Lionel Messi's enduring status as a symbol of unity and pride for Argentines during the World Cup.

entry.title: Belgian royals roll out red carpet for Japan's emperor
entry.content: Japanese Emperor Naruhito and Empress Masako received a ceremonial welcome at the Royal Palace of Brussels as they began a state visit to Belgium.

entry.title: UNAIDS warns Trump's HIV funding cuts to South Africa could cost lives
entry.content: The Trump administration says the phased withdrawal is linked to policy disputes with Pretoria, which rejects the allegations and says it is pursuing greater self-reliance.

entry.title: Iceland kills first whales since 2023, resuming whaling
entry.content: Iceland's Marine and Freshwater Research Institute has recommended that no more than 150 fin whales are caught in the 2026 season.

entry.title: Japan's emperor and empress arrive in Belgium for state visit
entry.content: The arrival of Japan's emperor and empress in Belgium marks a symbolic moment for two countries whose partnership stretches back more than a century and a half.

entry.title: Von der Leyen to visit Pashinyan in Yerevan to build on Armenia’s pro-EU vote
entry.content: As the EU rolls out a support package for Armenia, European Commission President von der Leyen is set to meet re-elected Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan in Yerevan after he secured a decisive pro-West mandate from the Armenian people, sources confirmed to Euronews.

entry.title: UN launches Hormuz evacuation for 11,000 stranded seafarers
entry.content: The operation follows a peace agreement between the United States and Iran that ended months of conflict and allowed commercial shipping to resume through one of the world's most important maritime routes.

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | June 23rd, 2026 – Evening
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 23rd, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: EU officials discreetly meet Taliban in Brussels to speed up Afghan deportations
entry.content: EU officials and Taliban representatives held a technical meeting in Brussels to discuss the return of Afghan nationals in a fresh attempt to ramp up repatriations of migrants without the right to stay in Europe.

entry.title: From Bolsonaro and Bukele to Fujimori and De la Espriella: Trump and Latin America's rightward turn
entry.content: Soon after the US president first moved into the White House, his policies and outspoken backing for certain candidates inspired a new wave of Latin American leaders now shaping the continent’s future.

entry.title: LEGO's Julia Goldin named first-ever European CMO of the Year at Cannes Lions
entry.content: Chosen by more than 800 marketing leaders from across Europe, Julia Goldin was recognised for helping transform LEGO into a global cultural brand through partnerships spanning entertainment, gaming and sport.

entry.title: Armenia and Azerbaijan agree landmark internet deal as peace process continues
entry.content: Armenia and Azerbaijan’s leading telecom companies agreed to a joint internet transit project as both countries move forward together after decades of war, boosting regional connectivity.

entry.title: Versailles unveils gallery for 250th anniversary of US independence
entry.content: Opening on Saturday 4 July, this gallery brings together paintings, sculptures and portraits from the château’s collections to spotlight the political, diplomatic and military figures of independence.

entry.title: How Israel became a test case for the EU's institutional battle over foreign policy
entry.content: Diverging views on the EU's relations with Israel are exposing a growing rivalry between the European Commission and top diplomat Kaja Kallas over who sets the bloc's foreign policy, undermining its overall coherence.

entry.title: Spain toughens rules for e-scooters: new requirements
entry.content: The government has approved a reform to the General Traffic Regulations making helmets compulsory for e-scooter users nationwide and setting a minimum age of 15. The measure forms part of a road safety package.

entry.title: Ukraine says key Crimea rail bridge ‘no longer exists’ after drone strikes
entry.content: Ukraine said it struck a major blow to Russia’s grip on the occupied Crimean peninsula, announcing the destruction of a key railway bridge over the North Crimean Canal in a series of strikes. The bridge is the “first one” to be eliminated, Kyiv’s troops said.

entry.title: Kubilius: Europe should prepare for ‘more’ attacks following suspected targeted killing in Poland
entry.content: The EU’s defence chief has cautioned that Moscow will continue provoking Europe as it falters on the frontlines, particularly after the high-profile death of an exiled Russian citizen critical of Putin living in Poland.

entry.title: At least 20 drownings reported as 54 French departments remain on red alert
entry.content: After an exceptionally hot day yesterday, with temperatures above 40°C in some cities, the heatwave continues: 54 départements are on red alert this Tuesday 23 June in France.

entry.title: Fire at animation studio in India kills at least 15 people
entry.content: The fire happened in a building housing a student training centre in northern India, raising fresh concerns about fire safety in the country.

entry.title: Poland-Ukraine row is 'not helpful' and plays into Putin's hands, Brussels warns
entry.content: Brussels has issued a stark warning as tensions between the Polish and Ukrainian presidents escalate. "There is only one happy observer in this type of situation, and that's the aggressor," the European Commission said.

entry.title: Floptropica: How much do you know about Gen Z's favourite country?
entry.content: If you're looking for a place to go this summer and escape the worries of everyday life, we've got you covered. However, before you book a ticket, there is just one small problem...

entry.title: Japan launches ‘supreme class’ bullet train airline-style private cabins
entry.content: Already renowned for their speed and comfort, Japan's Shinkansen bullet trains are about to become even more luxurious, with new private suites set to launch this autumn featuring lockable doors, reclining seats and dedicated Wi-Fi.

entry.title: Remarkably well preserved: Second World War assault gun uncovered
entry.content: During construction at the Nordholz naval air base on the North Sea, workers uncovered a near-intact 29-tonne StuG III assault gun from World War II, buried in sand for 80 years.

entry.title: Exceptionally good condition: Second World War assault gun found
entry.content: During construction work at the Nordholz naval air base on the North Sea, workers uncovered a surprise: an almost intact 29‑tonne StuG III assault gun from WWII, buried in the sand for 80 years.

entry.title: Exceptional condition: World War II assault gun found
entry.content: During construction work at Nordholz naval air base on the North Sea, workers unearthed an almost intact WWII StuG III assault gun that had lain hidden in the sand for 80 years.

entry.title: Kenya orders immediate halt to controversial US-backed Ebola facility
entry.content: The project had sparked court proceedings, public protests and questions about the country's role in responding to an outbreak beyond its borders.

entry.title: Czech president appeals to Constitutional court amid clash over NATO summit
entry.content: The 2026 NATO summit is set to be held on the 7 and 8 July at the Beştepe Presidential Compound in Ankara.

entry.title: Iran sharply contradicts the US, pushing its own view of framework deal
entry.content: Shortly after the first high-level US-Iran negotiations ended, discrepancies emerged over what the two sides actually agreed, as Tehran moved to directly contradict US Vice President JD Vance’s statements, prompting US President Donald Trump to reject Iran's claims.

entry.title: 40 mayors worldwide unite to fight back against the AI data centre surge
entry.content: From Phoenix to Melbourne, a global coalition of city leaders says the AI data centre boom is draining their power, water and land — and they are done waiting.

entry.title: Lithuania PM Ruginienė resigns after less than a year in office
entry.content: Prime Minister Inga Ruginienė resigned on Tuesday together with her cabinet, ending a ten-month tenure. She is to return to government as social affairs minister in a new cabinet expected to be led by Mindaugas Sinkevičius — the third Social Democrat prime minister since the 2024 elections.

entry.title: Newly uncovered Mozart manuscript gets world premiere in France
entry.content: The French National Library recovered a previously unknown manuscript by the world-famous Austrian composer, bringing to light seven new pieces for flute and harp.

entry.title: Russia brands Europe a ‘threat to peace’ but says it’s ready to talk
entry.content: Moscow said the US is no longer trying to be an "objective mediator" in its efforts to broker an end to Russia’s all-out war against Ukraine.

entry.title: TV pundit apologises after racist comment during Belgium World Cup game
entry.content: Bogdanović most notably played for La Liga side Atlético Madrid and Germany's Werder Bremen during his career.

entry.title: 2026 edition of Edinburgh International Festival to be a ‘phone-free’ environment
entry.content: An increasing amount of touring artists are demanding their concerts be phone-free. Now, Edingburgh's annual celebration of the performing arts is also asking audience members to put their phones away during the performances.

entry.title: AI cyber threat is 'months, not years' away, Western intelligence agencies warn
entry.content: Five Eyes warn of cyber threat ‘within months’ after US locks down Anthropic's AI models

entry.title: Spain sounds the alarm on the future of EU green cash
entry.content: Ahead of an environment ministers’ meeting in Brussels, Spain warns that the EU risks undermining its credibility on climate and biodiversity unless it protects dedicated funding tools that have delivered environmental results for more than three decades.

entry.title: Montenegro's Adriatic port of Bar eyed as potential Middle Corridor link
entry.content: Montenegrin President Jakov Milatović made a historic first official visit to Kazakhstan to discuss trade, transport routes, tourism and diplomatic relations.

entry.title: Former SNP chief Peter Murrell jailed for embezzling more than £400,000 of party funds
entry.content: "Murrell occupied a significant position of privilege and power in public life in Scotland," assistant chief constable Stuart Houston of Police Scotland said.

entry.title: Eurostar cancels some trains between Paris and London amid heatwave
entry.content: Two Eurostar services a day between the French and UK capitals have been scrapped due to 'adverse weather'.

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | June 23rd, 2026 – Midday
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 23rd, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: Who is the mysterious ‘Hot Podium Guy’ – the viral face of the UK’s political turmoil?
entry.content: UK Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer’s resignation only meant one thing for some – the mysterious “Hot Podium Guy” was back...

entry.title: Kazakhstan seeks 'much more' in ties with EU as Tokayev visits Brussels
entry.content: With Tokayev’s strategic visit to Brussels under way, Kazakhstan's ambassador to the EU tells Euronews in an exclusive interview that Astana is seeking even closer ties with the 27-member bloc, highlighting energy, critical minerals and AI ambitions.

entry.title: Australian town crier breaks Guinness record for world's loudest person
entry.content: Cover your ears and meet Joseph McGrail-Bateup, Canberra's official town crier and the new record-holder for the loudest ever shout.

entry.title: Labour MPs mull leadership challenge after Starmer resigns
entry.content: Former Health Secretary Wes Streeting has ruled himself out of a leadership race after he announced his backing for Andy Burnham.

entry.title: Seven injured, including two children, after vehicle mounts bar terrace in Malaga
entry.content: The incident occurred when a car ploughed into several customers on a bar terrace in the Malaga town. Police are investigating whether the driver suffered a health problem at the wheel.

entry.title: Cybercriminals target Portuguese citizens with fake confidentiality agreements in PM’s name
entry.content: The statement was circulated to editorial offices late on Monday night, reaching newsrooms shortly before midnight, according to the organisation.

entry.title: Céline Dion teases new song ahead of concert comeback
entry.content: After a sold-out concert residency and a first single, Céline Dion has teased a new song to be released on 3 July.

entry.title: Alpine glacier melts faster as second heatwave hits France
entry.content: The Bossons Glacier in France’s Chamonix Valley is showing fresh signs of rapid retreat as a second heatwave of 2026 grips parts of Europe.

entry.title: Europe heatwave: These countries have issued red heat alerts as continent swelters
entry.content: Travellers should be aware that high-level bulletins have been issued across France, Spain, Italy, Switzerland, Luxembourg and the UK.

entry.title: Venice Film Festival unveils 2026 jury with Maggie Gyllenhaal as president
entry.content: There are a lot of familiar Venice Film Festival faces on this year's jury roster. It may not be the starriest line-up, but it's certainly an eclectic group...

entry.title: Watch: French jets salute US anniversary with Washington flyover
entry.content: France's elite Patrouille de France aerobatic team performed a ceremonial flyover above some of Washington's most iconic sites as part of celebrations marking 250 years of American independence.

entry.title: Brexit: What would Britons vote for in a new referendum?
entry.content: New poll says the majority of Brits want the UK back in the bloc, but the next general election might crush EU hopes again.

entry.title: Police officer and civilian killed in Montreal shooting
entry.content: Montreal Police named the officer killed in the shooting as 34-year-old Mohamed Lamine Benredouane.

entry.title: Watch: Lies, damn lies, and statistics—the data about Brexit ten years on
entry.content: Exactly ten years ago today, the UK made a historic choice that permanently reshaped Europe. However, Britons do not need another European to tell them what they want. Instead, your continental reporter has prepared something far more British: a review.

entry.title: Brussels residents cool off in fountains as heatwave grips Belgium
entry.content: People flocked to fountains in Brussels' Parc du Cinquantenaire as temperatures climbed during a heatwave expected to intensify later this week.

entry.title: Messi extends World Cup goal record in win over Austria as France, Norway advance to knockout rounds
entry.content: Argentina, France and Norway advance to the knockout stages as Messi, Mbappe and Haaland continue to shine, scoring a brace each in their second fixtures of the group stage in a tightly contested race for the World Cup Golden Boot.

entry.title: Newsletter: Brexit at 10 — the divorce that still defines Britain
entry.content: Also in this newsletter: What happens now to the upcoming EU-UK Summit, who pays for Europe's return hubs, and a Commissioner's travel plans finally revealed.

entry.title: Nancy Guthrie ransom note said she died after abduction
entry.content: Guthrie went missing from her home in Tucson, Arizona, in early February. The FBI is offering a reward up to $100,000 for information leading to her location.

entry.title: Europe Today: Starmer's resignation, Brexit and the Poland-Ukraine row
entry.content: Keir Starmer announced his resignation as UK prime minister, while Britain marked 10 years since the Brexit referendum. Relations between Poland and Ukraine remained strained as Brussels welcomed Kazakhstan's president for talks on closer cooperation.

entry.title: Mandarin Oriental’s latest hotel just opened in Mallorca – and you can book eclipse experiences now
entry.content: Guests at the 131-key property can sign up for a hot air balloon ride as the island plunges into darkness, or watch the celestial event from a private yacht.

entry.title: How China’s currency makes the EU's trade deficit worse – and what Brussels can do
entry.content: EU leaders last week pointed to the undervaluation of the yuan as a major factor in the EU’s record trade deficit with China, which Brussels is taking steps to address.

entry.title: Pay transparency: Where in Europe can you see how much your colleagues earn?
entry.content: Employees in Italy, Malta, Slovakia, and Lithuania can now see salary details, and job postings must include pay information. Most EU countries have not implemented the Transparency Directive, leading to inconsistent compliance. Can you see salary details in your country?
